questions.question: Several EC2 instances across AZs need to read and write the same set of files simultaneously. Which storage service fits?
questions.feedback: EFS is a shared file system many instances can mount at once. EBS attaches to a single instance; instance store is ephemeral.

questions.question: A read-heavy relational database is struggling under query load. Which change improves read performance?
questions.feedback: Read replicas offload read traffic. Multi-AZ is for availability/failover, not read scaling.

questions.question: A global audience is downloading the same static images and videos with high latency. What helps most?

answers.text: Serve the content through Amazon CloudFront
Move everything to a single larger EC2 instance

questions.feedback: CloudFront caches content at edge locations near users, cutting latency and offloading the origin.

questions.question: You need microsecond read latency specifically for a DynamoDB table without rewriting the app. What do you add?
questions.feedback: DAX is the in-memory cache built specifically for DynamoDB.

questions.question: Which compute choices reduce operational overhead by removing server management?

answers.text: Self-managed EC2 instances you patch yourself

questions.feedback: Lambda (functions) and Fargate (serverless containers) remove host management. Self-managed EC2 keeps that burden with you.
